title: Remove all loading states for iframe tabs

requirements.text: Remove all loading spinners, loading overlays, and loading state indicators from the iframe tab browsing experience. When a URL is loaded in a tab, the iframe must appear immediately with no loading screen, no spinner, and no in-progress visual state. This applies to TabBar.tsx (remove per-tab loading indicators), TabContent.tsx (remove the loading overlay/spinner shown while the iframe loads), and useTabs.ts (remove the isLoading/loading state tracking for tabs entirely or stop using it to gate rendering).

quotes: make NO loading for any website!

acceptanceCriteria: Opening any URL in a proxy iframe tab shows no loading spinner or overlay at any point.
The tab bar shows no loading indicator on any tab while a site is loading.
The iframe is rendered immediately when a URL is set, without waiting for an onLoad event to hide a spinner.
No loading state (isLoading, loading, etc.) is used to conditionally render or hide the iframe content.
The error state (all proxies failed) is still shown if all proxy attempts fail — only the loading UI is removed, not error handling.

constraints: Do not remove the error state UI — if all proxy fallbacks fail, the error message and direct-link fallback must still be shown.
Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Changing proxy logic or fallback order.
Modifying the AI Assistant loading spinner (that is unrelated to iframe tabs).
Removing loading states from the Code Editor page.
